ab_table.best: 36.61 tok/s @ 100% resident (size_vram/size=1.0, vram-fit; peak VRAM 61.65 GiB, GTT 0.02, sys 5.45)
ab_table.delta: ~207x fresh end-to-end (36.61 vs 0.177); ~6.4x vs §11.1 clean ref (36.8 vs 5.7)
ab_table.lever: Residency: forced -vram (num_gpu=999, use_mmap=false) vs auto layer-estimate
ab_table.measured: FRESH end-to-end (both sides today; p8 + stack co-resident)
ab_table.why: Ollama auto-estimate sizes GPU layers to the 31 GiB OS system RAM, not the 96 GiB VRAM carveout, so a 64 GiB MoE is 62% CPU-offloaded; offloaded weights exceed system RAM and page from the 98%-full disk -> thrash. Forcing full residency pins 100% in the carveout.

ab_table.baseline: 43.92 tok/s agg @ c8 (flat ~43 across c1..8); TTFT p95 20444 ms @ c8
ab_table.best: 121.10 tok/s agg @ c8 (knee, TTFT p95 826 ms); ceiling 128.66 @ c16; scales 42->69->104->121->129
ab_table.delta: ~2.76x throughput @ c8; ~2.9x ceiling (128.66 vs 43.92); ~25x better TTFT p95 @ c8
ab_table.lever: Concurrency: OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=8 vs default 1 (7B workhorse qwen2.5:7b)
ab_table.measured: FRESH end-to-end (real container toggle p1<->p8, both today)
ab_table.why: One decode slot (p1) serializes -> aggregate stays flat while TTFT grows with the queue. 8 slots scale throughput to the LPDDR5X bandwidth plateau; knee at c8.

ab_table.best: exact-repeat hit ~1-2 ms; semantic-0.92 hit ~0.7-0.9 s
ab_table.measured: REUSED §7.1/§7.5; live config confirmed stores.semantic_cache enabled:true (not mutated)
ab_table.why: A hit skips the upstream LLM leg; an exact-repeat pre-routing cache skips embed+classify+routing entirely.
llama.cpp(rocm) is the fastest / lowest-TTFT server measured on gfx1151.

ab_table.lever: Architecture: MoE vs dense for big models
ab_table.why: MoE reads only the active experts per token, so bandwidth-bound decode is far faster than a same-size dense model.

ab_table.delta: ~1.7x decode, ~30 GiB VRAM saved, quality flat
ab_table.why: Fewer bytes/token -> faster bandwidth-bound decode; MMLU-Pro flat within the 42Q noise.

ab_table.baseline: dense 70B-Q4 0.0381 tok/s/W (5.07 @ 133 W, §12); naive gpt-oss default ~0.007 tok/s/W (0.177 @ ~24 W, derived)
ab_table.lever: Per-watt: resident MoE vs inefficient big-model config
ab_table.why: Socket pins ~100-133 W under load; efficiency tracks throughput, so the 100%-resident MoE wins ~10x per token. A CPU-offloaded model stalls the pipeline and wastes energy.

config_restore.ollama_num_parallel: recreated container -> =8 for BEST, then RESTORED to original (env unset -> server default OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL:1); env + restart policy + network + devices + volume verified identical to pre-run inspect; 15 models intact (named volume 'ollama').
config_restore.vllm_sr_cache: NOT changed (read-only inspection only) -> nothing to restore.

baseline_naive_default.residency: server auto layer-estimate (NO num_gpu / use_mmap override)
baseline_naive_default.semantic_cache: off (no route-local cache plugin on the tested decision)

environment.disk: 98% full (25 GiB free) -- only pre-present models used, nothing pulled
environment.gpu: Ryzen AI Max+ 395 (gfx1151); 96 GiB VRAM carveout, 30.99 GiB OS system RAM, 48 GiB GTT
environment.gpu_discipline: strictly sequential, one model loaded at a time (sole worker)
environment.stack: vllm-sr 9 containers + ollama co-resident; router /config/hash UP on :8080 during every rung

baseline.note: ~61.7% CPU-offload; offloaded weights exceed the 31 GiB OS system RAM so they page from the 98%-full disk -> mmap thrash

a_residency_decode.delta: 36.61 vs 0.177 tok/s = ~207x fresh end-to-end (baseline thrashes under 98%-full disk); residency 100% vs 38.3%; ~6.4x vs the §11.1 clean-run reference (36.8 vs 5.7)

b_concurrency.toggle: real container recreate: OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL unset(=1) -> =8 -> restored to unset(=1)

baseline_derived.note: derived from fresh baseline decode / spot socket-power reading during the CPU-offloaded decode; a full power_sampler run was skipped (0.18 tok/s => >10 min/run thrash).

notes: All FRESH numbers measured on Halo-B (test001-stxh) on 2026-07-13, GPU strictly sequential, full vllm-sr stack co-resident.

Full BEST config validated end-to-end: gpt-oss:120b-vram holds 36.61 tok/s @ 100% residency AND qwen2.5:7b p8 holds ~121-129 tok/s WITH OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=8 set and the 9-container stack co-resident -- no regression vs per-lever runs.

Baseline gpt-oss:120b default decode is highly variable with memory pressure: 0.177 tok/s this run (98%-full disk -> offload pages from disk) vs 3.06 tok/s prior clean run vs 5.7 tok/s §11.1 reference; residency ~0.38-0.41 either way.

cache_ttft.live_config_check: stores.semantic_cache enabled:true (memory, max_entries 1000, ttl 3600s); route-local plugin coverage varies -- NOT mutated this run

supersedes: bestcfg-ab/v1 (2026-07-13T16:26Z) -- this run adds the real OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL container toggle (p1<->p8) and fresh residency/per-watt.
title: BEST vs BASELINE end-to-end A/B (Halo-B, 96 GiB carveout, headless, full vllm-sr stack co-resident) -- full BEST config validated with ALL levers ON together on 2026-07-13
